text.data-narration: The primary objective of clustering is to identify patterns, structures, or groupings in the data that are not inherently present.

g.text: Clustering is a fundamental concept in machine learning that involves grouping similar data points into clusters.

text.data-narration: There are two primary types of clustering algorithms: Hierarchical Clustering and Non-Hierarchical Clustering.

g.text: There are two primary types of clustering algorithms:

text.data-narration: The most common proximity measures are Euclidean Distance, Minkowski Distance, Manhattan Distance, and Cosine Similarity.

g.text: Proximity measures are used to determine the similarity between data points.

text.data-narration: Hierarchical clustering algorithms build a hierarchy of clusters by merging or splitting existing clusters. There are two primary hierarchical clustering algorithms: Agglomerative Clustering and Divisive Clustering.

g.text: Hierarchical clustering algorithms build a hierarchy of clusters by merging or splitting existing clusters.

text.data-narration: k-means clustering is a type of non-hierarchical clustering algorithm that groups data points into clusters based on their proximity to the cluster center.

g.text: k-means clustering is a type of non-hierarchical clustering algorithm that groups data points into clusters based on their proximity to the cluster center.

text.data-narration: In conclusion, clustering is a powerful technique for identifying patterns and structures in data. By understanding the different types of clustering algorithms and proximity measures, you can develop effective clustering solutions for your machine learning projects.

g.text: In conclusion, clustering is a powerful technique for identifying patterns and structures in data.
